title: Big Lawsuit Over a Dirty River
summary: A big river called the River Wye is in trouble. It is turning green and smells bad. Many people who live and work near the river are very upset. They say two companies are making the river dirty. One company raises many chickens, and the other company provides water. The chicken company has a lot of chickens, and their poop is put on fields. When it rains, the poop washes into the river. The water company sometimes lets sewage spill into the river too. This makes the river green and smelly. More than 4,500 people are taking the companies to court. They want the companies to clean up the river and pay for the damage. The companies say they did not do anything wrong. A court will decide what happens. This is the biggest case about pollution in the UK.

keywords.explanation: when something harmful gets into the air, water, or land
a case in court where people ask a judge to solve a problem
a big stream of water that flows across the land
poop from chickens that is used to help plants grow
dirty water from toilets and sinks
a place where a judge listens to problems and makes decisions

background_read: The River Wye is one of the longest rivers in the UK. It is home to many animals and plants. People enjoy fishing, swimming, and walking near it. Recently, the river has become dirty because of too many nutrients from chicken poop and sewage. This causes algae to grow, which turns the water green and smells bad. Many people are worried about the health of the river.
Article_Structure: WHO: More than 4,500 people who live or work near the River Wye are suing two companies: Avara Foods and Welsh Water.
WHAT: The lawsuit claims the companies polluted the river with chicken manure and sewage, making it green and smelly.
WHERE: The River Wye, Lugg, and Usk along the border of England and Wales.
WHY: The pollution harms the river and affects people's lives and businesses.

perspectives.description: They want the river cleaned and compensation for damage to their lives and businesses.
They say the claims are wrong and they did not cause the pollution.
